feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Given a Customer Account set up as a ship-to partner function of another account find that sold-to
entry.content:
In SAP HANA I have a customer account, call it 1001234 - it represents a customer, with many ship-to locations. So it is my sold-to account number.

Also I have an account, call it 5009876 and it represents one specific ship-to location/address.

In 1001234, under "Sales and Distribution" in the Partner Functions table, I assign 5009876 as a Ship-To party of 1001234.

I do not maintain anything in the new "Relationships" section of 1001234

Is it possible, by looking at the screens for account 5009876 to know that it was assigned as a ship to partner function of 1001234?

This was possible in ECC using the "Customer Partner Functions" choice in the "Extras" menu when viewing 5009876.

entry.title: Partial stock transfer of QI stock
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

following situation:

I post the PGR of 10 hobbocks in our external warehouse(A) which runs into QI-stock.

For a quality inspection i want to move only 1 hobbock with a stock transfer to my warehouse(B) as best all quantities remains the whole time in QI. An UD is obligated at the end.

Its all within the same plant, only 2 different stor.locs.

At the moment i have two options: 

1. A complete pre-release of the inspection LOT to unrestricted and moving (MIGO) 1 hobbock from "A" to "B", but then the whole quantity is in unrestricted although its not physically QI-approved and could be used by mistake. This is the current procedure.

2. Use the QAC2 to move the complete quantity based on the LOT while its in QI, but thats makes no sense for my issue, because i need just 1 hobbock in "B".

Maybe s.o. have a smart and easy solution to handle this.

Thanks in advance and br,

Simon

entry.title: Assistance Required for SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) & Scale‑Up High Availability Configuration
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have completed the installation of the SAP HANA database on both the primary node (currently running with business data) and the secondary node (with no business data).

I am seeking your assistance in configuring SAP PERSON_NAME System Replication (HSR) and setting up a Scale‑Up High Availability (HA) environment for our SAP HANA database.

At present, neither the system replication nor the HA setup has been configured. We request your guidance and support to complete the configuration according to SAP best practices.

We request SAP’s support for the following activities:

  1. Guidance on configuring SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) between the primary and secondary nodes.
  2. Assistance with the correct configuration sequence, including:
    • Initial data load
    • Secondary node registration
    • Activation of replication
    • Validation and health checks
  3. Recommendations for Scale‑Up High Availability setup, including best practices for:
    • Replication mode (sync / syncmem / async)
    • Operation mode (logreplay)
    • Cluster integration with Pacemaker (if applicable)
  4. Verification that the planned architecture is aligned with SAP recommendations and supported configurations.
  5. Clarification on any prerequisite checks, logs, or additional information required from our side to proceed.

We are ready to provide diagnostic logs, system replication outputs, landscape information, or any other details upon request.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Hana SQL Command Network Protocol Deprecation/Sunset
entry.content: I remember reading somewhere that the Hana SQL Command Network Protocol might be getting shut down in the next few years, but I was unable to find that information when I went looking for it. As the new products move away from using that protocol and some of the official drivers on GitHub are no longer being supported (Python), does anyone know of any plans to shut down that protocol for future versions of the Hana product? If so, is there a definitive timeline for that?

entry.title: SAP BASIS associate certification exam after the sap migration
entry.content: Hi all, am planning for an SAP BASIS associate certification.. what are the courses to cover and exams i should write? has the exam released in new format?  can someone confirm? thanks

entry.title: How to have multiple schedules in service call whenever there is a contract?
entry.content:
Hi,
I'm unable to add another schedule when the service call has a contract linked to it.
The service call is still open, and I can enter the schedule details in the row, but it won’t save.
I tested another service call without a contract, and in that case I can add additional schedules without any issue.
The contract is active (not terminated), and in the Document Settings for Service Calls, the option “Scheduling of multiple tickets” is enabled. I also have full authorization for the Service Call module.

Can anyone help me? 
Thanks

entry.title: Enable Offline Store for Online OData Service in SAP MDK
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently using an online OData service ( S/4HANA) - SSAM2405, but I would like to enable an offline store option so that functions like updates can be performed offline, similar to how offline services work.

This screenshot shows the offline service

Is there a way to enable offline store directly for an online OData service? If not, are there any alternatives or best practices to use an online service in a local/offline store?

Any guidance or suggestions would be highly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
Kingston A.

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP  , SAP Field Service Management, connector for SAP Business One  
#SAP Service and Asset Manager,   #SAP Mobile Services,  #SAP Mobile Asset Management , SAP BTP, Kyma runtime  , SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud  SAP HANA service for SAP BTP  , SAP Field Service Management, connector for SAP Business One  
#SAP Service and Asset Manager,   #SAP Mobile Services,  #SAP Mobile Asset Management , SAP BTP, SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud

entry.title: CREATE OR REPLACE TYPE
entry.content:
How do I create or replace a type in HANA 2.0 SP5?

I want to create a type if it currently does not exist. How do I do that?

entry.title: Customer overwritten during CO-PA derivation (SD to CO-PA mismatch)
entry.content:
Hi all!

I’m struggling with a derivation issue where the Customer (KUNNR) is being overwritten during the billing release to CO-PA.

  • SD Billing (VF03): The customer is correctly set as Client A.

  • Profitability Segment (KE24): The customer is changed to Client B.

I have traced the derivation flow and found that the system moves a value from a custom field  into a CO-PA characteristic and then performs a Table Access to derive/overwrite the Customer.

Has anyone experienced a situation where standard SD customer data is aggressively overwritten by a KEDR Table Access?

Thank you!

entry.title: Impact of Material Type in MM01 on SAP Configuration and Processes
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to better understand the technical and functional impact of the Material Type defined in MM01.

From my understanding, material type controls several aspects of the material master and logistics processes, such as:

  • Number range assignment

  • Allowed views in the material master

  • Procurement type

  • Quantity/value updating

  • Account determination in FI

  • Field selection in material master

  • Integration with other modules

However, I would like to understand in more detail:

  1. Which SAP tables and configuration objects are directly influenced by the material type?

  2. How does the material type affect valuation, accounting, and stock management behavior?

  3. What is the relationship between material type and account determination (OBYC)?

  4. How does it influence procurement processes (purchasing vs production)?

  5. Are there any best practices for defining custom material types (Z material types) in SAP MM?

I would appreciate a deeper explanation of the technical and configuration impact of material type within SAP MM and its integration with FI and other logistics modules.

entry.title: How to Avoide production confirmation consume stock in wrong SLoc
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts

Our company have 2 different SLoc for the materials, one for Storage Warehouse, one for Production SLoc in the plant

When our production operators confirm the production order, they sometimes consume the stock in Storage SLoc, when there is not enough inventory in the production SLoc. 

Can someone advice a solution to avoide production operators to consume the stock from wrong SLoc ?

entry.title: Cannot restore SAP HANA Database using lob backups
entry.content:
Is it possible restore an SAP PERSON_NAME using only Log backups?


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

With SNAP Centes:

  1. Restore SNAP
  2. Restore Log backups to a local File System
  3. Restore PITR point in time recovery using log backups

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: MultiLevel BOM Report for all Materials
entry.content:
Hello,

For manufacturing and budget purposes, we need to have a table with all the MultiLevel BOMs, including the semi-finished goods.

As of now, we have built it in our external data warehouse, but we want to study the feasibility of generating this data in SAP.

Does anybody has any feedback regarding this topic?

We are in S4/HANA on private cloud.

Could it be a CDS View? Should we build a table that we refresh regularly via an ABAP program? any other option?

Thank you

Julien

entry.title: Does HANA Planviz from HANA Studio execute a temporary procedure when analyzing a statement?
entry.content: I used Planviz from HANA Studio to generate a planviz of 2 complex select queries with parameters.  Later, I saw that the statements were logged in the HANA audit log as having been executed by a procedure.  Why did that happen?  I executed the statements from SQL Console and generated a PlanViz.

entry.title: How to force measurement point entry in PM order confirmation?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we assigned measuerment points to some of our functional locations to track run time for liquids ran through a pipe for example.

Is there a possibility to force the user to enter actual status of the measurement point (actual counter) during PM order confirmation or at least during completion of the PM order?

We have S4CORE (SAPK-10701INS4CORE).

As of now I did not found anything excepts development what I cannot believe that this is the only way.

thanks in advance.

Best regards

Patrick

entry.title: PPDS Process Order Not Visible in Fiori Confirmation App Despite Correct Dates
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA (Process Manufacturing with PPDS integration) and would like your guidance.

Scenario

  • A Process Order was created from a planned order (sales order driven requirement).

  • Sales order delivery date (VA03): 11.03.2026

  • In /SAPAPO/RRP3, requirement date (Avail/ReqD): around 20.03.2026

  • Process order header basic start date: 20.03.2026

However, at phase/operation level:

  • Scheduled (Earliest) Start Date: 28.03.2026 18:30

  • Scheduled Finish Date: 28.03.2026 20:00

Issue

  • The order was NOT visible in the Fiori confirmation app

  • Later identified that the app considers phase scheduled dates (not header dates)

  • Since the phase was scheduled on 28.03.2026, it was outside the app’s default selection range

Additional Observations

  • Resource, plant, and user mapping are correctly maintained

  • Operation quantities and statuses are valid (REL, DSPT, etc.)

  • No issue with authorization or master data

  • Order is now confirmed, so scheduling log is not available

Concern

Even though:

  • Sales order requirement date = 11.03.2026

  • Planning requirement in PPDS ≈ 20.03.2026

  •  The system scheduled the phase on 28.03.2026

 Questions

  1. How can we identify the exact reason (capacity, setup, material, etc.) that caused PPDS to reschedule the order to 28.03.2026?

  2. Is there any way to trace or log the scheduling decision after the order is already confirmed?

Any inputs or recommended debugging approaches would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Tabela de vínculo de número de série em esboço (Draft) de NF de Saída no SAP Business One
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Preciso identificar em qual tabela é realizado o vínculo entre item da linha, e número de série em esboço de NF de Saída.

Quando selecionamos o número de série, e adicionamos a NF de Saída como esboço, ao fechar a tela e voltar no esboço, o número de série segue atribuído ao item, o que significa que isso fica registrado em alguma tabela.
Qual seria?

 

 

Encontrei apenas o vínculo para itens controlados por lote, que é por meio da tabela (DRF16 -> OBTN), quando estamos falando de esboço especificamente.
Para números de série não encontro.

Alguém poderia me ajudar? Por favor

Atte,

Gabriel Camargo

SAP Business One

entry.title: CALLING SAP HANA STORED PROCEDURES WITH VARIABLES
entry.content:
Community

I need to create a stored procedure that will be called from the query manager. I need to include variables so the user can input the values ​​they need, ideally dates and a subtype of an item. If I call the procedure with predefined values, it works, but I need it to be dynamic and allow the user to input that data.

If you could help me with how to structure this or how to call those variables from the query manager ([%0]), I would appreciate it.

entry.title: SAP backup of data for a specific company code
entry.content:
There is a requirement to back up all SAP transactions on daily basis and keep the data physically in a specific country, including both manual and automatic entries.
Can the HANA database back up data for a specific company code? Or is there another way to achieve this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How an I traverse thru tables and fill a result table?
entry.content:
BusinessOne objects in SAP rest in a bunch of tables, that have a numeric ID but a text name - for whatever reason.
The first function just gets me the correct table:

CREATE FUNCTION "q_GetDocLinesTable"
/**
 * Get B1 objects table of a documents lines
 * V1.0 Coded 2026-02-05 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	/* B1 Document Type */
	IN ObjType int
)
	/* B1 Document Meta Table */
	RETURNS TableName NVARCHAR(10)

	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 

AS

BEGIN

	IF     (:ObjType=13) THEN TableName := 'INV1'; /* A/R Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=14) THEN TableName := 'RIN1'; /* A/R Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=15) THEN TableName := 'DLN1'; /* Delivery */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=16) THEN TableName := 'RDN1'; /* Returns */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=17) THEN TableName := 'RDR1'; /* Sales Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=18) THEN TableName := 'PCH1'; /* A/P Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=19) THEN TableName := 'RPC1'; /* A/P Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=20) THEN TableName := 'PDN1'; /* Goods Receipt PO */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=21) THEN TableName := 'RPD1'; /* Goods Return */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=22) THEN TableName := 'POR1'; /* Purchase Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=23) THEN TableName := 'QUT1'; /* Sales Quotation */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=24) THEN TableName := 'RCT1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=67) THEN TableName := 'WTR1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=540000006) THEN TableName := 'PQT1'; /* WTF? */
	ELSE TableName := NULL;
	END IF;
End;

I also created a type that I need to fill:

CREATE TYPE "q_tt_EntryMap" AS TABLE (
	"Entry" INTEGER,
	"Type" INTEGER
);

Now, what I want to is:

  • Step 1: Start with any given combination of Entry/Type
  • Step 2: fill a resulting table of type q_tt_EntryMap with the combination (if found)
  • Step 3: if any "BaseEntry": move to that combination and goto Step 2.

The following function gets me the first entry, but I'm unsure how to append to my result-table and how to move on to the next entry:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_GetEntryTable"
(
EntryID int,
EntryType int
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
	tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
	Result        "q_tt_EntryMap";

BEGIN

	LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);
--	WHILE (:LineTable IS NOT NULL)
--	BEGIN
		tSQL := '
		SELECT DISTINCT "BaseEntry" AS "Entry", "BaseType" AS "Type"
		FROM ' || :LineTable || '
		WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryID || ' AND "BaseType" <> -1
		';
		EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL into Result; /* ## How do I append? ## */
--		LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(/* ## Somehow put Type-Field of last row of :result into it ## */);


--	END;
		
	SELECT * FROM :Result;

END;

 

So if [1234,15] has a BaseEntry of [2345,13] and that has a BaseEntry of [3456,17] (I just made up those numbers), the resulting table returned should look like this:

(Entry)(Type)
123415
234513
345617

Of course, if there is a better way to do this, please feel free to tell me.
